domain: industrial thermography (infrared fault detection in electrical equipment)
specific_mechanism: Thermography: passive infrared imaging maps temperature distribution across a piece of equipment; anomalous hotspots locate latent faults before they cause failure. Basin-stability framing: each operating state is a basin in (temperature × current × load) phase space; thermography measures distance from a basin boundary.
llm_application: Activation-thermography for LLMs: probe a sequence of counterfactual prompts; map per-layer activation L2-norm shift versus the unperturbed prompt. Hotspots = neurons crossing a decision-basin boundary; basin-stability is measured by smallest perturbation that flips output. Used to predict latent prompt-injection vulnerability before it causes a jailbreak.
novelty_claim: Activation-patching and counterfactual probing are well-known interpretability techniques. Whether the explicit 'basin-stability per neuron' framing — a dynamical-systems concept from ecology / network theory — is formalized in 2024-2026 LLM safety literature is the open question.
